brands.name: Wild Birds Unlimited
brands.notes: Leading specialty bird-feeding retail franchise founded in Indianapolis (1981) and franchising since 1983, HQ in Carmel, Indiana. ~340 franchised units across the US and Canada with a full Item 19 disclosing mean annual sales of roughly $858K.

riskFlags.title: Flat unit count in 2024
riskFlags.detail: Franchised unit count was unchanged year-over-year (340 in 2023 and 2024) after modest gains in prior years (+7 in 2023), and the franchisor added its first company-owned location. Steady, not high-growth.

riskFlags.title: Wide revenue dispersion
riskFlags.detail: Item 19 shows a very wide store sales range ($169,482 to $2,733,484), so unit economics vary substantially by location and market.

nationwide.source: Wild Birds Unlimited official franchise site - Available Markets in the USA (44 states with locations; nationwide development)
nationwide.note: Franchisor lists available markets across the USA; brand has locations in 44 states and franchises nationwide plus Canada.

franchiseFee.note: New franchisee fee; $20,000 for existing franchisee additional store, $5,000 for converting an independent store

adFundPercent.note: 1% national ad fund; additional local advertising requirement

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19 mean annual store sales (2024 data, 2025 FDD); median $773,456; range $169,482-$2,733,484

totalUnits.note: 340 franchised + 1 company-owned at end of 2024 per FDD; franchisor publicly cites ~363 including Canada

yearFounded.note: First store opened in Indianapolis in 1981

multiUnitAvailable.note: Reduced $20,000 fee for existing franchisees opening additional stores

brands.name: Sky Zone
brands.notes: Indoor trampoline park pioneer founded 2004, franchising since 2009; ~193 units (126 franchised, 67 company-owned) per 2024 FDD. Multi-million-dollar buildout business in the active-entertainment sector with inherent injury-liability exposure.

riskFlags.title: Personal-injury litigation exposure (sector-wide)
riskFlags.detail: Trampoline parks including Sky Zone face recurring personal-injury lawsuits over supervision, waiver enforceability, and equipment maintenance; reported Sky Zone suits in 2018 and 2020. Catastrophic pediatric injury cases are an active plaintiff-bar focus across the category.
riskFlags.source: Penney & Associates / Attorney911 trampoline injury litigation overviews

riskFlags.title: High capital bar
riskFlags.detail: Total investment of roughly $2.18M to $4.72M with $500,000 liquid capital and $1.8M net worth minimums; large-format real-estate-dependent buildout.

riskFlags.title: No/limited Item 19 financial performance representation
riskFlags.detail: Some analyses indicate Sky Zone does not make a full Item 19 earnings disclosure; third-party revenue figures are estimates, not FDD-sourced earnings claims.

nationwide.source: Sky Zone official franchise page - territories available across 320+ markets; franchising nationwide
nationwide.note: Franchisor states 270+ parks in 320+ US markets with territories still available; franchises sold nationwide (US and Canada).

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2024 FDD Item 7; varies with 16,000-50,000 sq ft park size

totalUnits.note: 126 franchised + 67 company-owned per 2024 FDD; franchisor publicly cites 270+ parks currently

veteranDiscount.note: 20% off initial franchise fee for veterans/active-duty military

brands.name: Urban Air Adventure Park
brands.notes: Large-format indoor adventure park brand owned by Unleashed Brands; ~193 franchised units with a full Item 19 (2.0 parks averaging ~$3.19M gross sales). Among the highest-capital entertainment franchises, with a history of franchisee disputes and injury litigation.

riskFlags.title: Major injury arbitration award
riskFlags.detail: Damion Collins v. Urban Air Overland Park produced a $15.6M arbitration award (Sept 14, 2023) in which the arbitrator found the signed waiver NOT enforceable, citing systemic safety-information failures. Catastrophic-injury exposure is a material risk for the format.

riskFlags.title: Franchisee litigation over undisclosed costs
riskFlags.detail: A group of ~50 franchisees (Adventure Park Franchisee Association) sued Urban Air alleging post-PE-acquisition changes added untenable, undisclosed costs. The franchisor prevailed on standing grounds, but the dispute signals franchisee-relations friction.

riskFlags.title: Franchisee bankruptcy / closures
riskFlags.detail: A Fredericksburg, VA Urban Air franchisee (Two Fish Partners) filed Chapter 11 in 2025, illustrating that individual unit-level financial failures occur in this high-capex model.

riskFlags.title: Very high capital bar
riskFlags.detail: Total investment of ~$3.11M to $5.79M with $750,000 liquid capital and $1.5M net worth required per the franchisor's development site.
riskFlags.source: Urban Air official franchise investment page

nationwide.source: Urban Air official franchise development site (USA Franchise Opportunities + Canadian Territories)
nationwide.note: Franchisor markets opportunities across the USA and Canada; offers nationwide territory availability.

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2025 FDD Item 7 range $3.554M-$8.332M per verification audit 2026-06-11. Prior batch value 3111409 superseded.

totalInvestmentMax.note: 2025 FDD Item 7 range $3.554M-$8.332M per verification audit 2026-06-11. Prior batch value 5791969 superseded.

adFundPercent.note: Up to 6% local marketing (currently 5%); NAF up to 5% currently 0%

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19 2.0-park average gross sales (123 units, 2024 data in 2025 FDD); avg EBITDA $613,352 (19.2%). 2.5-park avg sales $4,233,365.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Markets 2.0 and 2.5 park formats and multi-park development

brands.name: Altitude Trampoline Park
brands.notes: Dallas-based indoor trampoline/adventure park brand founded 2013, franchising since 2016; ~80 units (69 franchised, 11 company-owned) per 2025 FDD. High-capital active-entertainment format with category-typical injury-liability exposure.

riskFlags.title: Personal-injury litigation exposure (sector-wide)
riskFlags.detail: Altitude parks have faced injury suits (e.g., a 2018 New Jersey case over a child's leg injury) centered on maintenance and supervision; the trampoline-park category broadly draws catastrophic-injury litigation and waiver-enforceability challenges.

riskFlags.title: High capital bar
riskFlags.detail: Total investment of roughly $1.63M to $2.96M including franchise fee; large-format leased-real-estate buildout.

nationwide.source: Altitude Trampoline Park official franchising site
nationwide.note: Franchisor markets franchise opportunities nationally and internationally; reported double-digit annual openings.

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2024 FDD Item 7; figures vary by FDD version

franchiseFee.note: Sources range $40,000-$50,000 across FDD versions; most recent cite $50,000

avgUnitRevenue.note: Reported average yearly gross sales per FDD-derived analysis; represents gross sales not profit

multiUnitAvailable.note: Area development option with multi-park commitments offered

brands.name: Hampton by Hilton
brands.notes: Upper-midscale hotel brand under Hilton, founded 1984 and franchising since 1984; 3,200+ properties globally (2,200+ in the US) and Entrepreneur's #1 hotel franchise for over a decade. A multi-million-dollar, real-estate-intensive hotel-development franchise, not a small-business format.

riskFlags.title: Hotel-scale capital requirement
riskFlags.detail: Total investment is hotel-development scale: roughly $15.2M to $22.2M (broader range $6.9M-$22.2M across sources) with ~$3.0M liquid capital, a 22-year agreement term, and land/construction risk. This is an institutional-grade investment, not an owner-operator small business.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Investor Data / PeerSense (2023-2024 Hampton FDD)

riskFlags.title: High ongoing fee load
riskFlags.detail: Combined ongoing fees total ~10% of gross rooms revenue (6% royalty + 4% program/marketing fee covering Hilton Honors, CRS, and technology), standard for upper-midscale but a meaningful drag on margins.

nationwide.source: Hilton corporate development site - Hampton by Hilton US franchise development (2,200+ US franchises)
nationwide.note: Hilton actively develops Hampton hotels nationwide across the US (2,200+ US properties) plus Canada and 46 countries internationally.

totalInvestmentMin.note: More recent FDD-derived range; broader cross-source range starts ~$6.9M

franchiseFee.note: Sources cite $75,000 base; some cite $100,000 - varies by number of rooms (per-key minimums apply)

adFundPercent.note: 4% program/marketing fee (Hilton Honors, CRS, technology, marketing)

totalUnits.note: 3,200+ properties in 46 countries; 2,200+ in the US

yearFounded.note: Launched 1984 as Hampton Inn by Holiday Corporation

isGrowing.note: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 #1 hotel franchise for 14+ consecutive years; continued global expansion
